items.text:
Hi Marcus,

Your resume review with Career Services is confirmed for Thursday, March 12, at 2:00 PM in Building C, Room 118. Please bring two printed copies. A $5 printing credit is available at the front desk if you still need copies made.

Best,
Ms. Reyes

questions.stem: According to the email, when is Marcus's resume review scheduled?

options.A: On Thursday at two in the afternoon
options.B: On Thursday at noon that same day
options.C: On Friday at two in the afternoon
options.D: On Wednesday at ten in the morning

questions.explanation: The email sets the appointment for Thursday, March 12, at 2:00 PM, which option A restates as Thursday at two in the afternoon.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who still need printed copies?

options.A: They must cover a steep printing fee alone
options.B: They cannot make any copies on campus
options.C: They can offset the cost at the desk
options.D: They should email the resume to reschedule

questions.explanation: Because a $5 printing credit is offered at the front desk, a student needing copies can offset part of the cost there, supporting option C.

items.text:
Dear Priya,

Thanks for signing up to volunteer as a campus tour guide. Your first shift is Saturday, April 5, from 10:00 AM to noon. Please arrive at the front steps of the Welcome Center about ten minutes early. Remember to wear the blue volunteer shirt.

Thanks,
The Dean's Office

questions.stem: According to the email, where should Priya go for her shift?

options.A: To the steps outside the main library
options.B: To the steps at the Welcome Center
options.C: To the entrance of the dining commons
options.D: To the lobby inside the recreation building

questions.explanation: The email tells Priya to arrive at the front steps of the Welcome Center, which is option B.

questions.stem: According to the email, what should Priya wear for the shift?

options.A: A blue shirt worn by volunteers
options.B: A formal business outfit for the tour
options.C: A plain white shirt of her choosing
options.D: A printed paper name tag and lanyard

questions.explanation: The email asks Priya to wear the blue volunteer shirt, which option A paraphrases as a blue shirt marking her as a volunteer.
